---
title: 'A fragile recovery: Rural employment set back twice since 2007'
description: Relative change in total employment for rural and nonrural counties indexed
  to a 2007 pre-recession baseline.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-3-1-emp-change-since-gr-lc/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 3.1_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: 'A fragile recovery: Rural employment set back twice since 2007'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Tracks total employment relative to a 2007 pre-recession baseline for rural and nonrural counties through 2024. Both groups lost jobs during the Great Recession and COVID-19, but rural areas entered 2024 still below parity while nonrural counties had recovered and continued growing.](./3.1_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

Tracks total employment relative to a 2007 pre-recession baseline for rural and nonrural counties through 2024. Both groups lost jobs during the Great Recession and COVID-19, but rural areas entered 2024 still below parity while nonrural counties had recovered and continued growing.

## Key Findings

- Rural employment had not fully recovered to 2007 levels as of 2024 — the index remains below 1.0.
- Nonrural employment surpassed the 2007 baseline by 2015 and continued to grow.
- Both series show a sharp COVID-19 dip in 2020 followed by rapid recovery, with rural lagging.
- The Great Recession shaded band (2007-2009) anchors the starting point of divergence.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/emp_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
